nativeRange.habitat: Steep outer reef drop-offs, rubble areas, and among growths of black coral and sea fans; typically found at depths of 22–80 m

diet.notes: In the wild, has been documented consuming worms, bottom-dwelling invertebrates, and plankton. In captivity, generally accepts finely chopped crustacean flesh and mysis shrimp; may require coaxing initially.

imageCandidates.notes: Photographer: D. Terver, Muséum-Aquarium de Nancy. Non-commercial use with attribution.

summary: The Mitratus Butterflyfish is a striking deep-reef species from the Western Indian Ocean, recognized by its yellow body with two diagonal black stripes and a distinctive eye mask. Generally considered intermediate in care and best kept in a large, well-decorated aquarium as a mated pair.
careNotes: The Mitratus Butterflyfish (also sold as Indian Butterflyfish or Black and Yellow Butterflyfish) is a deepwater species typically found at 22–80 m depth along steep outer reef drop-offs among black coral and sea fans. It tends to be peaceful with other fish but is not reef-safe, as it has been known to nip at corals and ornamental invertebrates. In the wild, small groups of up to 5 individuals have been observed; in captivity it is generally advised to keep singly or as a mated pair. The species has been documented forming monogamous pair bonds and is best purchased and kept as an established pair. Feeding can require some coaxing — offer finely chopped crustacean flesh, mysis shrimp, and varied frozen foods. Ample live rock with open swimming space is recommended. Temperature data shows some disagreement between LiveAquaria (22–26°C converted from 72–78°F) and FishBase modeled habitat data (24–27°C); the ranges were averaged to 23–26.5°C.
breedingNotes: Oviparous; forms monogamous breeding pairs in the wild. Captive breeding has not been reliably documented for this species.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (14.0 cm SL vs LiveAquaria 5.5 in/14 cm — consistent), native range, habitat depth, temperature (24.4–26.9°C vs LiveAquaria 72–78°F / 22.2–25.6°C — minor spread, averaged to 23–26.5°C), taxonomy, and grouping behavior. Reef2Reef wiki returned 403; BlueZoo and Fishkeeping World had no species page — FishBase used as sole confirmed secondary.

fish.breedingNotes: Oviparous; monogamous pair bonds observed in the wild. No reliable captive breeding records.
